verdict.answer_first: B: opens with the answer immediately: "Server-side rendered — or more precisely, prerendered/static — not client-side." A also answers first, but adds softer hedge: "almost certainly".
verdict.decisiveness: B: commits harder with "not client-side" and "this isn't close"; A weakens the call by turning into a route-by-route survey/table.
verdict.fabrication: A overclaims with "fastest possible load, perfect SEO"; B overclaims with "weaker for ranking" without evidence. A's absolutes are more inflated.
verdict.actionability: B: clearer prescription: prerender/static by default, runtime SSR only for per-request parts, client interactivity on top.
